title: The Marketing Meeting
summary: Joe Furnari, Jason Branham, Michael Donovan, Matt Watson, and Vinnie Micciche opened the meeting with a brief discussion on extreme weather, and Harinder Bhinder joined the call. Michael Donovan introduced a three-lane marketing approach—Lane One: "Truth" (dealer visibility), Lane Two: "Control" (speed flow and follow-up engineering), and Lane Three: "Vision" (AI plus human integration)—intended to structure all messaging, with Vinnie Micciche pressing for specifics on the execution plan, and Jason Branham suggesting a focus on actions to restore high-volume lead generation. The participants discussed defining the core message of Lane One as "you can't manage what you can't see," aligning existing content with these lanes, balancing messaging for dealership "grunts" and decision-makers interested in AI and efficiencies, and agreed to use the three lanes as an architecture to anchor strategic outcomes of "trust," "belief," and "preference," respectively.

decisions.description: Agreed to use the three lanes as an architecture for strategic outcomes.
Three lanes will structure all messaging across funnel, website, and social posts.
Three lanes to serve as messaging principles for content production efforts.

actionItems.task: Develop three core messages before the next meeting.
Share V20 group decks for review and feedback.
Create a visual for the three lanes, core messaging, and features.
Review and provide feedback on Vinnie Micciche's shared decks.

tensions: Vinnie Micciche's push for execution specifics over framework.
Concern over balancing messaging for different dealership roles.
Anxiety regarding the rapid pace of AI development in the industry.
referencesToPast: Reference to past high-volume lead generation success.
Mention of existing V20 group presentation decks.
